units.sanskrit: श्रीभगवानुवाच मय्यासक्तमना: पार्थ योगं युञ्जन्मदाश्रय: । असंशयं समग्रं मां यथा ज्ञास्यसि तच्छृणु ॥ १ ॥
units.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca | mayy āsakta-manāḥ pārtha yogaṁ yuñjan mad-āśrayaḥ | asaṁśayaṁ samagraṁ māṁ yathā jñāsyasi tac chṛṇu || 1 ||
units.translation: Śrī Bhagavān said: O Pārtha, hear how you, having the mind fixed on Me, practising the Yoga of Meditation and taking refuge in Me, will know Me with certainty and in fullness.

units.sanskrit: ज्ञानं तेऽहं सविज्ञानमिदं वक्ष्याम्यशेषत: । यज्ज्ञात्वा नेह भूयोऽन्यज्ज्ञातव्यमवशिष्यते ॥ २ ॥
units.transliteration: jñānaṁ te ’haṁ sa-vijñānam idaṁ vakṣyāmy aśeṣataḥ | yaj jñātvā neha bhūyo ’nyaj jñātavyam avaśiṣyate || 2 ||
units.translation: I shall tell you in detail of this Knowledge which is combined with realization, after experiencing which there remains nothing else here to be known again.

units.sanskrit: मनुष्याणां सहस्रेषु कश्चिद्यतति सिद्धये । यततामपि सिद्धानां कश्चिन्मां वेत्ति तत्त्वत: ॥ ३ ॥
units.transliteration: manuṣyāṇāṁ sahasreṣu kaścid yatati siddhaye | yatatām api siddhānāṁ kaścin māṁ vetti tattvataḥ || 3 ||
units.translation: Among thousands of men, a rare one endeavours for perfection. Even of the perfected ones who are diligent, one perchance knows Me in truth

units.sanskrit: भूमिरापोऽनलो वायु: खं मनो बुद्धिरेव च । अहङ्कार इतीयं मे भिन्ना प्रकृतिरष्टधा ॥ ४ ॥
units.transliteration: bhūmir āpo ’nalo vāyuḥ khaṁ mano buddhir eva ca | ahaṅkāra itīyaṁ me bhinnā prakṛtir aṣṭadhā || 4 ||
units.translation: This Prakṛti of Mine is divided eight-fold thus: earth, water, fire, air, space, mind, intellect and also egoism.

units.sanskrit: अपरेयमितस्त्वन्यां प्रकृतिं विद्धि मे पराम् । जीवभूतां महाबाहो ययेदं धार्यते जगत् ॥ ५ ॥
units.transliteration: apareyam itas tv anyāṁ prakṛtiṁ viddhi me parām | jīva-bhūtāṁ mahā-bāho yayedaṁ dhāryate jagat || 5 ||
units.translation: O mighty-armed one, this is the inferior (Prakṛti). Know the other Prakṛti of Mine which, however, is higher than this, which has taken the form of individual Ātman(s), and by which this world is upheld.

units.sanskrit: एतद्योनीनि भूतानि सर्वाणीत्युपधारय । अहं कृत्स्नस्य जगत: प्रभव: प्रलयस्तथा ॥ ६ ॥
units.transliteration: etad-yonīni bhūtāni sarvāṇīty upadhāraya | ahaṁ kṛtsnasya jagataḥ prabhavaḥ pralayas tathā || 6 ||
units.translation: Understand thus that all things (sentient and insentient) have these as their source. I am the origin as also the end of the whole Universe.

units.sanskrit: मत्त: परतरं नान्यत्किञ्चिदस्ति धनञ्जय । मयि सर्वमिदं प्रोतं सूत्रे मणिगणा इव ॥ ७ ॥
units.transliteration: mattaḥ parataraṁ nānyat kiñcid asti dhanañ-jaya | mayi sarvam idaṁ protaṁ sūtre maṇi-gaṇā iva || 7 ||
units.translation: O Dhanañjaya, there is nothing else whatsoever higher than Myself. All this is strung on Me like pearls on a string.

units.sanskrit: रसोऽहमप्सु कौन्तेय प्रभास्मि शशिसूर्ययो: । प्रणव: सर्ववेदेषु शब्द: खे पौरुषं नृषु ॥ ८ ॥
units.transliteration: raso ’ham apsu kaunteya prabhāsmi śaśi-sūryayoḥ | praṇavaḥ sarva-vedeṣu śabdaḥ khe pauruṣaṁ nṛṣu || 8 ||
units.translation: O son of Kuntī, I am the taste of water, I am the effulgence of the moon and the sun; (the letter) ĀŪṀ in all the Vedas, sound in space, and manhood in men.

units.sanskrit: पुण्यो गन्ध: पृथिव्यां च तेजश्चास्मि विभावसौ । जीवनं सर्वभूतेषु तपश्चास्मि तपस्विषु ॥ ९ ॥
units.transliteration: puṇyo gandhaḥ pṛthivyāṁ ca tejaś cāsmi vibhāvasau | jīvanaṁ sarva-bhūteṣu tapaś cāsmi tapasviṣu || 9 ||
units.translation: I am also the sweet fragrance in the earth; I am the brilliance in fire, and the life in all beings; and I am the austerity of the ascetics.

units.sanskrit: बीजं मां सर्वभूतानां विद्धि पार्थ सनातनम् । बुद्धिर्बुद्धिमतामस्मि तेजस्तेजस्विनामहम् ॥ १० ॥
units.transliteration: bījaṁ māṁ sarva-bhūtānāṁ viddhi pārtha sanātanam | buddhir buddhimatām asmi tejas tejasvinām aham || 10 ||
units.translation: O Pārtha, know Me to be the eternal Seed of all beings. I am the intellect of the intelligent, I am the courage of the courageous.

units.sanskrit: बलं बलवतां चाहं कामरागविवर्जितम् । धर्माविरुद्धो भूतेषु कामोऽस्मि भरतर्षभ ॥ ११ ॥
units.transliteration: balaṁ balavatāṁ cāhaṁ kāma-rāga-vivarjitam | dharmāviruddho bhūteṣu kāmo ’smi bharatarṣabha || 11 ||
units.translation: And of the strong I am the strength which is devoid of passion and attachment. Among creatures I am desire which is not contrary to righteousness, O scion of the Bhārata dynasty.

units.sanskrit: ये चैव सात्त्विका भावा राजसास्तामसाश्च ये । मत्त एवेति तान्विद्धि न त्वहं तेषु ते मयि ॥ १२ ॥
units.transliteration: ye caiva sāttvikā bhāvā rājasās tāmasāś ca ye | matta eveti tān viddhi na tv ahaṁ teṣu te mayi || 12 ||
units.translation: Those things that indeed are made of (the quality of) sattva, and those things that are made of (the quality of) rājas and tamas, know them to have sprung from Me alone. However, I am not in  them; they are in Me!

units.sanskrit: त्रिभिर्गुणमयैर्भावैरेभि: सर्वमिदं जगत् । मोहितं नाभिजानाति मामेभ्य: परमव्ययम् ॥ १३ ॥
units.transliteration: tribhir guṇa-mayair bhāvair ebhiḥ sarvam idaṁ jagat | mohitaṁ nābhijānāti mām ebhyaḥ param avyayam || 13 ||
units.translation: All this world, deluded as it is by these three things made of the guṇas (qualities), does not know Me who am transcendental to these and undecaying.

units.sanskrit: दैवी ह्येषा गुणमयी मम माया दुरत्यया । मामेव ये प्रपद्यन्ते मायामेतां तरन्ति ते ॥ १४ ॥
units.transliteration: daivī hy eṣā guṇa-mayī mama māyā duratyayā | mām eva ye prapadyante māyām etāṁ taranti te || 14 ||
units.translation: Since this divine Māyā of Mine which is constituted by the guṇas is difficult to cross over, (therefore) those who take refuge in Me alone cross over this Māyā.

units.sanskrit: न मां दुष्कृतिनो मूढा: प्रपद्यन्ते नराधमा: । माययापहृतज्ञाना आसुरं भावमाश्रिता: ॥ १५ ॥
units.transliteration: na māṁ duṣkṛtino mūḍhāḥ prapadyante narādhamāḥ | māyayāpahṛta-jñānā āsuraṁ bhāvam āśritāḥ || 15 ||
units.translation: The foolish evildoers, who are the most depraved among men, who are deprived of (their) wisdom by Māyā, and who resort to demoniacal ways, do not take refuge in Me.

units.sanskrit: चतुर्विधा भजन्ते मां जना: सुकृतिनोऽर्जुन । आर्तो जिज्ञासुरर्थार्थी ज्ञानी च भरतर्षभ ॥ १६ ॥
units.transliteration: catur-vidhā bhajante māṁ janāḥ su-kṛtino ’rjuna | ārto jijñāsur arthārthī jñānī ca bharatarṣabha || 16 ||
units.translation: O Arjuna, foremost of the Bhārata dynasty, four classes of people of virtuous deeds adore Me: the afflicted, the seeker of Knowledge, the seeker of wealth and the man of Knowledge.

units.sanskrit: तेषां ज्ञानी नित्ययुक्त एकभक्तिर्विशिष्यते । प्रियो हि ज्ञानिनोऽत्यर्थमहं स च मम प्रिय: ॥ १७ ॥
units.transliteration: teṣāṁ jñānī nitya-yukta eka-bhaktir viśiṣyate | priyo hi jñānino ’tyartham ahaṁ sa ca mama priyaḥ || 17 ||
units.translation: Of them, the man of Knowledge, endowed with constant steadfastness and one-pointed devotion, excels. For I am very much dear to the man of Knowledge, and he too is dear to Me.

units.sanskrit: उदारा: सर्व एवैते ज्ञानी त्वात्मैव मे मतम् । आस्थित: स हि युक्तात्मा मामेवानुत्तमां गतिम् ॥ १८ ॥
units.transliteration: udārāḥ sarva evaite jñānī tv ātmaiva me matam | āsthitaḥ sa hi yuktātmā mām evānuttamāṁ gatim || 18 ||
units.translation: All of these, indeed, are noble, but the man of Knowledge is the very Self. (This is) My opinion. For, with a steadfast mind, he is set on the path leading to Me alone who am the super-excellent Goal.

units.sanskrit: बहूनां जन्मनामन्ते ज्ञानवान्मां प्रपद्यते । वासुदेव: सर्वमिति स महात्मा सुदुर्लभ: ॥ १९ ॥
units.transliteration: bahūnāṁ janmanām ante jñānavān māṁ prapadyate | vāsudevaḥ sarvam iti sa mahātmā su-durlabhaḥ || 19 ||
units.translation: At the end of many births the man of Knowledge attains Me, (realizing) that Vāsudeva is all. Such a high Ātman is very rare.

units.sanskrit: कामैस्तैस्तैर्हृतज्ञाना: प्रपद्यन्तेऽन्यदेवता: । तं तं नियममास्थाय प्रकृत्या नियता: स्वया ॥ २० ॥
units.transliteration: kāmais tais tair hṛta-jñānāḥ prapadyante ’nya-devatāḥ | taṁ taṁ niyamam āsthāya prakṛtyā niyatāḥ svayā || 20 ||
units.translation: People, deprived of their wisdom by desires for various objects and guided by their own nature, resort to other deities following the relevant methods.

units.sanskrit: यो यो यां यां तनुं भक्त: श्रद्धयार्चितुमिच्छति । तस्य तस्याचलां श्रद्धां तामेव विदधाम्यहम् ॥ २१ ॥
units.transliteration: yo yo yāṁ yāṁ tanuṁ bhaktaḥ śraddhayārcitum icchati | tasya tasyācalāṁ śraddhāṁ tām eva vidadhāmy aham || 21 ||
units.translation: Whichever form (of a deity) any devotee wants to worship with faith, that very firm faith of his I strengthen.

units.sanskrit: स तया श्रद्धया युक्तस्तस्याराधनमीहते । लभते च तत: कामान्मयैव विहितान्हि तान् ॥ २२ ॥
units.transliteration: sa tayā śraddhayā yuktas tasyārādhanam īhate | labhate ca tataḥ kāmān mayaiva vihitān hi tān || 22 ||
units.translation: Being imbued with that faith, that person engages in worshipping that form, and he gets those very desired results therefrom as they are dispensed by Me alone.

units.sanskrit: अन्तवत्तु फलं तेषां तद्भ‍वत्यल्पमेधसाम् । देवान्देवयजो यान्ति मद्भ‍क्ता यान्ति मामपि ॥ २३ ॥
units.transliteration: antavat tu phalaṁ teṣāṁ tad bhavaty alpa-medhasām | devān deva-yajo yānti mad-bhaktā yānti mām api || 23 ||
units.translation: That result of theirs who are of poor intellect is indeed limited. The worshippers of gods go to the gods. My devotees go to Me alone.

units.sanskrit: अव्यक्तं व्यक्तिमापन्नं मन्यन्ते मामबुद्धय: । परं भावमजानन्तो ममाव्ययमनुत्तमम् ॥ २४ ॥
units.transliteration: avyaktaṁ vyaktim āpannaṁ manyante mām abuddhayaḥ| paraṁ bhāvam ajānanto mamāvyayam anuttamam || 24 ||
units.translation: The unintelligent, unaware of My supreme state which is immutable and unsurpassable, think of Me as the unmanifest that has become manifest.

units.sanskrit: नाहं प्रकाश: सर्वस्य योगमायासमावृत: । मूढोऽयं नाभिजानाति लोको मामजमव्ययम् ॥ २५ ॥
units.transliteration: nāhaṁ prakāśaḥ sarvasya yoga-māyā-samāvṛtaḥ | mūḍho ’yaṁ nābhijānāti loko mām ajam avyayam || 25 ||
units.translation: Being enveloped by Yoga-māyā, I do not become manifest to all. This deluded world does not know Me who am birthless and undecaying.

units.sanskrit: वेदाहं समतीतानि वर्तमानानि चार्जुन । भविष्याणि च भूतानि मां तु वेद न कश्चन ॥ २६ ॥
units.transliteration: vedāhaṁ samatītāni vartamānāni cārjuna | bhaviṣyāṇi ca bhūtāni māṁ tu veda na kaścana || 26 ||
units.translation: O Arjuna, I know the past, the present, as also the future beings; but no one knows Me!

units.sanskrit: इच्छाद्वेषसमुत्थेन द्वन्द्वमोहेन भारत । सर्वभूतानि सम्मोहं सर्गे यान्ति परन्तप ॥ २७ ॥
units.transliteration: icchā-dveṣa-samutthena dvandva-mohena bhārata | sarva-bhūtāni sammohaṁ sarge yānti paran-tapa || 27 ||
units.translation: O scion of the Bhārata dynasty, O destroyer of foes, due to the delusion of duality arising from likes and dislikes, all creatures become bewildered at the time of their birth.

units.sanskrit: येषां त्वन्तगतं पापं जनानां पुण्यकर्मणाम् । ते द्वन्द्वमोहनिर्मुक्ता भजन्ते मां दृढव्रता: ॥ २८ ॥
units.transliteration: yeṣāṁ tv anta-gataṁ pāpaṁ janānāṁ puṇya-karmaṇām | te dvandva-moha-nirmuktā bhajante māṁ dṛḍha-vratāḥ || 28 ||
units.translation: On the other hand, those persons who are of virtuous deeds, whose sin has come to an end, they, being free from the delusion of duality and firm in their convictions, adore Me.

units.sanskrit: जरामरणमोक्षाय मामाश्रित्य यतन्ति ये । ते ब्रह्म तद्विदु: कृत्स्नमध्यात्मं कर्म चाखिलम् ॥ २९ ॥
units.transliteration: jarā-maraṇa-mokṣāya mām āśritya yatanti ye | te brahma tad viduḥ kṛtsnam adhyātmaṁ karma cākhilam || 29 ||
units.translation: Those who strive by resorting to Me for becoming free from old age and death, they know that Brahman, everything about the individual Self, and all about actions.

units.sanskrit: साधिभूताधिदैवं मां साधियज्ञं च ये विदु: । प्रयाणकालेऽपि च मां ते विदुर्युक्तचेतस: ॥ ३० ॥
units.transliteration: sādhibhūtādhidaivaṁ māṁ sādhiyajñaṁ ca ye viduḥ | prayāṇa-kāle ’pi ca māṁ te vidur yukta-cetasaḥ || 30 ||
units.translation: Those who know Me as existing in the physical and the divine planes, and also in the context of the sacrifice, they of concentrated minds know Me even at the time of death.
